title: [CI:BUILD] Packit: Enable failure notifications for cockpit tests
body:
Configure packit to automatically notify relevant Cockpit team members when one of the "cockpit-revdeps" tests fails.
    
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] - This is test configuration.
    
Signed-off-by: Martin Pitt <mpitt@redhat.com>

See https://packit.dev/posts/weekly#week-37-september-12th--september-18th and https://packit.dev/docs/configuration/#message

To test this, I sent the first version with an [additional commit that breaks checkpointing](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/e57162d4982d9d5c4d4c0c51d633608bf5aa7e42). This created a notification comment, see below.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Cockpit tests failed for commit e57162d4982d9d5c4d4c0c51d633608bf5aa7e42. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5*, *martinpitt* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This is great!! I should add these for the packit build tests on all repos.

@martinpitt I guess no big deal for now but having commit sha could probably end up being a bother in case of a PR with multiple commits as that might trigger multiple notifications. I'm told packit will check if prior notification is the same if it exists, and if the message doesn't match it'll send another notification.

EDIT: Packit folks also mentioned the possibility of limiting the number of notifications if there's interest.

@lsm5 Multiple commits aren't the problem, packit only runs tests (and notifies) for the top commit, i.e. `/refs/pull/1234/head` (or `/merge`) The idea of mentioning the commit hash is that this is clickable, and statuses are attached to that -- that makes it easy to find the old logs after a PR gets force-pushed, and thus they disappear from the PR's "checks" list.

You can check it right here -- after my force push, the test commit disappeared, and all tests were green. But you can still click on https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/e57162d4982d9d5c4d4c0c51d633608bf5aa7e42 and follow the ❌ to get the failure logs for the test commit.

> @lsm5 Multiple commits aren't the problem, packit only runs tests (and notifies) for the top commit, i.e. `/refs/pull/1234/head` (or `/merge`) The idea of mentioning the commit hash is that this is clickable, and statuses are attached to that -- that makes it easy to find the old logs after a PR gets force-pushed, and thus they disappear from the PR's "checks" list.
> 
> You can check it right here -- after my force push, the test commit disappeared, and all tests were green. But you can still click on [e57162d](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/e57162d4982d9d5c4d4c0c51d633608bf5aa7e42) and follow the ❌ to get the failure logs for the test commit.

@martinpitt I got multiple notifications at https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20061
